overall_answer: No. In the current strengthened study, solar exposure does not improve out-of-sample RED onset prediction overall or in vulnerable country-years after political-economy controls, fixed-effect exposure designs, and shifted-timing placebo checks.

requirements.result: 5 causal-pathway steps define required evidence from solar forcing through predictive validation.
159 countries and 18285 country-years; controlled event geometry p=0.9516.
331 onsets in 17950 at-risk country-years; controlled onset geometry p=0.9721.

requirements.requirement: Test vulnerability interactions and vulnerable-country prediction
requirements.result: Vulnerable held-out subset: n=430, events=17, delta AUC=-0.0104, delta log-loss=0.1843, placebo p=0.5283.
24 predeclared rules; selected held-out balanced accuracy=0.5519.
Annual circular specificity p=0.1826; country-year shifted exposure p=0.9391.
RED annual controls stronger than SILSO: 26; country-year controls include V-Dem, WDI political economy, food production, and battle deaths.

requirements.requirement: Use monthly or seasonal timing where possible
requirements.result: 343 RED episodes have start months; strongest monthly lag=3m, r=0.06143, circular p=0.2898.
10 subtype onset screens; strongest subtype=Civil-resistance revolutions, geometry p=0.2065.
High geomagnetic-sensitivity onset p=0.4629; auroral-proximity onset p=0.9187.

requirements.requirement: Report whether solar predicts onset out-of-sample
requirements.result: Overall delta AUC=-0.01016, delta log-loss=0.5293; vulnerable delta AUC=-0.0104, vulnerable placebo p=0.5283.
